items.title: Record attendance for AFL Gather Round as premier warns of 'lite' interstate version

children.content: South Australia's premier says he is "trying to hold the line" as Gather Round negotiations continue, with the 2026 event attracting record crowds.

articles.title: 'He didn't want to speak to me': Port star speaks after report over language

children.content: Zak Butters defends himself after the Power star was reported for using inappropriate language towards an umpire.

image.altText: GettyImages-2270971785

articles.title: Interstate fans say keep Gather Round in Adelaide

children.content: Crows AFLW player Ebony Marinoff has questioned the AFL's CEO on why the women's program hasn't been part of the successful Gather Round in Adelaide.

image.altText: A packed crowd in football jerseys and scarves walking across a bridge across a river.

items.title: Handwritten church music 'caked in dust' performed after 50 years

children.content: A South Australian church celebrates 150 years with a special performance of sacred music transcribed by early-20th-century parishioners.

image.altText: A man with glasses and a blue coat picking up sheet music from inside a white, rusty chest

items.title: Independent supermarkets struggle to keep prices down as Iran war hurts

children.content: Suppliers are charging more to deliver groceries to supermarkets as fuel prices increase, putting pressure on smaller retailers.

image.altText: Fresh vegetables on three black shelves with price stickers in front of the items.

items.title: Nick Xenophon-founded SA Best party comes to an end

children.content: SA Best, the party formed by stunt-loving politician Nick Xenophon, has closed its chapter in South Australian political history with its final member Connie Bonaros losing her seat in last month's state election.

image.altText: A woman with curly blonde hair standing with a cut-out of a man wearing an orange vest

items.title: Hiker found 'safe and well' after spending 24 hours lost in Victoria's north-west

children.content: Police have located a hiker who separated from his group and was missing for more than 24 hours in Victoria's north-west.

image.altText: Police finds missing camper Andy

items.title: 'Epic in scale': APY Lands exhibition opens at NGA after three-year delay

children.content: An exhibition of First Nations art from the APY Lands in South Australia brings together 30 large-scale works by nearly 50 artists.

image.altText: Two woman sitting on while painting.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: The fourth-year med student from Broken Hill who won the Stawell Gift

children.content: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: Olufemi Komolafe holds the trophy after winning the 2026 Men's Stawell Gift

items.title: Milk prices may need to increase to cover soaring fuel and fertiliser costs

children.content: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

gallery.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

thumbnail.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

items.title: Bricks and brutalism being added to heritage list, as owners raise concerns

children.content: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

gallery.altText: A concrete building with a metal roof
A small brick church among gum trees
A brick and wood house among trees

A small brick church among gum trees
A brick and wood house among trees

items.title: SA GP with an 'unhealthy obsession' for laser therapy disqualified

children.content: An Adelaide doctor with an "unhealthy obsession" for low-level laser therapy has had his registration cancelled after a tribunal found he demonstrated a "blatant disregard" of previous orders imposed on his registration.

image.altText: Close-up photo of a silver and white stethoscope. Dec 2022.

items.title: Commuters trapped for hours in freeway gridlock blast lack of signage

children.content: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

image.altText: Cars fill a freeway

items.title: How did a 3km hike go so wrong? Retracing Celine Cremer's final steps

children.content: Belgian backpacker Celine Cremer was on the trip of a lifetime, but on a hike in Tasmania she left the trail and was never seen again. It took a private investigator, a YouTuber, loved ones and an army of volunteers to find answers.

gallery.altText: A woman in orange hi-vis pictured from below shines a torch while walking through dense, tall forest
Blonde woman with dark blonde wavey hair out, seated at a table. A brunette woman sits beside her, hand  rests on her cheek
Three people in long-sleeved, orange hi-vis clothing emerge from a dense green forest. They appear wet and muddy

items.title: Canada has flipped the script on child care. Could Australia copy?

children.content: Australia definitely won't nationalise child care, even though the data suggests there's a strong case. Perhaps a more feasible idea would be to copy what Canada has done.

image.altText: A child walks at childcare.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Underground 3D printing farms arming Ukraine's soldiers

children.content: In spare rooms and backyard sheds, volunteers across Europe are 3D printing drone bomb parts to supply Ukraine's military.

image.altText: A man in camoflague with his face covered sits between boxes of 3D printed military equipment.

items.title: Australia's best inventor you've never heard of

children.content: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

items.title: ASX slips as oil surges back above $US100 on US threat of Gulf blockade

children.content: Failed US-Iran peace talks send oil prices surging higher and the Australian share market lower. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Rory McIlroy joins exclusive group with second straight Masters victory

children.content: Rory McIlroy becomes just the fourth man in history to win back-to-back Masters titles after holding off a star-studded field in the final round at Augusta. Follow live.

items.title: Man agrees he tried to 'humiliate' prominent surfer before fatal assault

children.content: A man has admitted to stripping a prominent Sydney surfer naked and shaking him by his ankles in an attempt to humiliate him before causing his unlawful death.

image.altText: A middle-aged man wearing a cap smiles at the camera.

items.title: Memorial for women allegedly killed by men removed from Melbourne laneway

children.content: The founder of Australian Femicide Watch says she is heartbroken over the removal of the She Matters mural in Hosier Lane dedicated to more than 200 women who have died since January 2025.

image.altText: A person stands at the top of a ladder against a wall with women's pictures as a crowd gathers below.

items.title: Shock loss of Emily has friends saying 'get your moles checked'

children.content: Emily avoided the sun and "did everything right". No-one considered that a melanoma removed from her shoulder in 2022 had spread to her brain.

items.title: Farmer braces for backlash after speaking up in support of transmission lines

children.content: A farmer in Victoria's north is offering up his property for transmission lines as part of the VNI West project, saying it would give him a chance to diversify his income.

items.title: Children and 75yo among dozens of homeless in major holiday town

items.title: Outback road trip murder charge heard in NT court

children.content: NT Police have submitted more than 120 pieces of evidence in the case of a Queensland man who is charged with murdering his girlfriend on an outback road trip last week.

items.title: Hungary's Orbán era ends in a landslide election win for opposition

children.content: After 16 years in power, the powerful far-right leader says he will serve in opposition, with centre-right ⁠candidate Péter Magyar's Tisza Party ​on course for a super majority.

items.title: Nigerian military air strike on market reportedly kills more than 100 civilians

children.content: Amnesty International calls for an independent investigation into an air strike on a Nigerian market, where the country's military says it was targeting militants.

items.title: Bondi Junction security guard who confronted attacker awarded bravery medal

children.content: Two years on from the Westfield Bondi Junction stabbing attack, eight heroes have been recognised in a special Australian Bravery Decorations honours list.

image.altText: Muhammad Taha sitting on a grey couch wearing a black hoodie.

items.title: Fuel crisis has some delaying retirement and working from home

children.content: The COVID-19 pandemic brought job losses, working from home and economic instability. Some fear the US-Israel war on Iran and spiking fuel prices have created similar struggles.

image.altText: A woman sitting with a guitar looking away from the camera.

items.title: Community seeks to become sole managers of NSW national park in two years

children.content: The Wreck Bay Aboriginal Community Council  plans to take on sole management of Booderee National Park In May 2028.
